question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a staking-reward-bearing HCS topic with no authentication or authorization check, precondition: any valid topicId is known, trigger: call subscribeTopic() with that topicId, impact: attacker receives all staking reward messages published to that topic including reward amounts and account IDs?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged user bypass topic existence validation when grpcProperties.isCheckTopicExists() returns false, precondition: operator has disabled checkTopicExists, trigger: subscribe to a non-existent topicId that maps to a staking reward feed, impact: attacker receives a synthetic TOPIC entity and proceeds to drain staking reward messages without a real topic guard?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged user supply a topicId whose entityRepository.findById() returns an entity of type other than TOPIC, precondition: entity exists but is an ACCOUNT type holding staking reward state, trigger: filter() on EntityType.TOPIC fails and switchIfEmpty emits IllegalArgumentException, impact: attacker probes entity types to map staking reward account IDs without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the gap recovery path by crafting a subscription where last is null and current is null (safety check path), precondition: no prior messages received, trigger: safety check fires after 1 second calling missingMessages(topicContext, null), impact: topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() is called with the original filter's startTime exposing all historical staking reward messages from epoch 0?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the numMissingMessages calculation (current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1) by injecting a live message with a very large sequence number, precondition: attacker controls or predicts a live message on the topic, trigger: numMissingMessages becomes enormous, impact: topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() is called with a massive limit fetching all staking reward messages in the gap including unpaid reward records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user cause the duplicate-message suppression branch (numMissingMessages <= -1) to silently drop legitimate staking reward messages, precondition: attacker sends or replays a message with a sequence number lower than last, trigger: Flux.empty() is returned instead of the reward message, impact: legitimate subscriber never receives their staking reward notification causing effective theft by omission?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the limit recalculation (filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get()) to produce a negative limit value when count exceeds the original limit due to a race condition, precondition: concurrent message delivery increments count past limit before incomingMessages() reads it, trigger: negative limit passed to filter.toBuilder().limit(limit), impact: malformed filter causes retriever to return unbounded staking reward messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the startTime derivation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) when last is null to reuse the original filter.getStartTime() of 0, precondition: subscriber sets startTime=0 and no historical messages exist, trigger: incomingMessages() builds newFilter with startTime=0, impact: live listener receives all staking reward messages from the beginning of chain history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user open an unlimited number of concurrent subscriptions to a staking reward topic with no per-user rate limit or connection cap, precondition: no authentication required, trigger: attacker opens thousands of subscribeTopic() streams simultaneously, impact: subscriberCount.incrementAndGet() grows unboundedly exhausting server threads and denying legitimate staking reward subscribers service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set startTime=0 in TopicMessageFilter to retrieve the entire historical staking reward message history of a topic, precondition: @Min(0) allows startTime=0, trigger: topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(filter, true) fetches all records from consensus timestamp 0, impact: attacker exfiltrates complete staking reward payout history including all account IDs and amounts?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set limit=0 (hasLimit() returns false) to receive an unbounded stream of staking reward messages with no termination, precondition: limit=0 passes @Min(0) validation and hasLimit() returns false, trigger: flux.take() is never applied, impact: attacker receives infinite stream of all staking reward messages without any cap?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user prevent the subscription from completing by setting endTime to a far-future value, precondition: isComplete() returns false until endTime + endTimeInterval passes, trigger: subscription stays open indefinitely consuming server resources, impact: attacker holds open a persistent staking reward tap draining all future reward messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the condition filter.getEndTime() < startTime returning true immediately to cause isComplete() to return true before any messages are processed, precondition: attacker crafts endTime just above startTime then races to read messages before completion, trigger: timing window between flux construction and isComplete() check, impact: partial staking reward message set is delivered then stream closes hiding the incomplete delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged user cause an IllegalStateException by delivering out-of-order messages to onNext(), precondition: concurrent safetyCheck and live streams both deliver messages to the same TopicContext, trigger: onNext() throws IllegalStateException when isNext() returns false, impact: subscription crashes mid-stream causing legitimate staking reward messages to be lost and never redelivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the non-atomic check-then-act in onNext() where isNext() is checked and then last.set() is called separately, precondition: two concurrent threads deliver messages to the same TopicContext, trigger: TOCTOU race between isNext() and last.set(topicMessage), impact: duplicate staking reward messages are delivered to the subscriber inflating apparent reward amounts?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the filter() predicate (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) to receive duplicate messages when last is null, precondition: last is null at subscription start, trigger: filter returns true for all messages when last==null, impact: attacker receives all staking reward messages including those already processed by other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user craft a scenario where the gapFilter built in the safety check path uses the original filter's endTime (null) resulting in an unbounded gap recovery query, precondition: safety check fires with current==null and last==null, trigger: gapFilter has no endTime limit, impact: topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() returns all staking reward messages from startTime to present with no upper bound?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user trigger repeated safety check gap recoveries by keeping the subscription alive without receiving any messages, precondition: no live messages arrive, trigger: safetyCheck fires every second via Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1L)), impact: repeated unbounded retrieve() calls against the staking reward topic database causing data exfiltration and DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic used for staking reward notifications without any identity verification, precondition: gRPC endpoint is publicly accessible, trigger: subscribeTopic() called with known staking reward topicId, impact: attacker monitors all staking reward payouts in real time including victim account IDs and amounts enabling front-running or targeted attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user cause incomingMessages() to build a newFilter with startTime derived from a stale last.getConsensusTimestamp() due to a race with onNext(), precondition: concurrent message processing updates last between getLast() and filter construction, trigger: startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 uses stale value, impact: gap in staking reward messages is created causing missed reward notifications?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to Long.MAX_VALUE to create a subscription that never terminates and continuously receives staking reward messages, precondition: endTime validation only checks endTime > startTime, trigger: isComplete() never returns true because Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, Long.MAX_VALUE) is always in the future, impact: permanent open tap on staking reward topic consuming all server resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: TopicContext.rate()] Can an unprivileged user cause integer overflow in rate() when count.get() is very large and elapsed is small, precondition: attacker floods topic with messages causing count to grow large, trigger: (int)(1000.0 * count.get() / elapsed) overflows int range, impact: corrupted rate metric masks the attack in monitoring dashboards allowing sustained staking reward data exfiltration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) combination to receive the same staking reward message from both the safety check path and the live path before the deduplication filter runs, precondition: safety check and live stream both return the same message concurrently, trigger: merge emits both before filter(t -> last==null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) processes them, impact: duplicate staking reward messages delivered to subscriber?
